reasoning: The Lean formalization correctly captures the informal problem. The plane is encoded as `EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin 2)`, a punch's action is modeled via `Irrational (dist P Q)` matching "distance is irrational", and `S.encard = n` with n : ℕ forces S to be a finite set of exactly n points (since the coerced value in ℕ∞ is finite). The condition `∀ Q, ∃ P ∈ S, Irrational (dist P Q)` faithfully expresses "every point in the plane is removed by at least one punch". Taking `sInf` over ℕ gives the minimum such n (the set is nonempty since n = 3 works, and larger n also work by extension, so sInf = minimum). This is a clean, faithful encoding of the Putnam 1990 A4 problem.
